notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms are imam (this — specific), dharmyam (consistent with dharma), saṅgrāmam (battle / engagement), and pāpam (sin / demerit). The engineering layer preserves all four. The 'imam' specificity — flagged by both Shankara and Ramanuja as load-bearing — is preserved in the analog: 'three conditions hold together — the duty has already arrived in your lane, the work is genuinely role-aligned, and the obligation has been encoded in commitments others have made.' This mirrors Shankara's three-clause scoping ('presented itself as a duty, not opposed to righteousness, enjoined') and Ramanuja's 'immediate and incumbent' framing. The pāpam is correctly mapped to 'organisational debt the team cannot easily discharge' rather than to a moralistic 'wrongdoing' frame. The kīrti-loss is preserved as 'technical reputation that depended on shipping under pressure.' The verse is not flattened to 'never quit a job' — the falsifiability and counter_example explicitly carve out the legitimate-exit cases. PASS.
